title: senecio sarracenicus subsp. fluviatilis
intro: Senecio sarracenicus subsp. fluviatilis, also known as River Ragwort or Alpine Ragwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family and is native to mountainous regions in Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. It is also believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Herbal preparations of Senecio sarracenicus subsp. fluviatilis may be used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, but proper identification and dosing are crucial due to the plant's potential toxicity.
